name: Atenolol
indications:
## Main indications

Selective beta-blocker. Antihypertensive. Beta-adrenergic antagonist.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.presentation: Tablets 50 and 100 mg. Ampoule 5 mg/10 mL (limited IV use).
adulto.dose: HTN/angina: 50–100 mg/day PO. Arrhythmias: per cardiology protocol.
adulto.administration: PO in single or divided doses.

pediatrico.dose: Children: initial: 0.5 to 1 mg/kg/day every 12-24 h; maximum dose: 2 mg/kg/day, up to 100 mg/day. Adults: 25-50 mg/day every 24 h, maximum dose: hypertension: 100 mg/day; angina: 200 mg/day.
pediatrico.notes: Avoid abrupt discontinuation. Use with caution in patients with bronchospasm, diabetes mellitus, hyperthyroidism. Initial doses of antihypertensives are tentative and should be modified according to clinical response and side effects. See preliminary guide for prevention of teratogenesis caused by medications.

adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Fatigue, lethargy, hallucinations, bradycardia, hypotension, rash, nausea, vomiting.
